meta.query: (all:"document parsing" OR all:"document understanding" OR all:"optical character recognition" OR all:OCR OR all:"layout analysis" OR all:"document layout analysis" OR all:"text recognition" OR all:"table recognition" OR all:"form understanding" OR all:"document intelligence" OR all:"page understanding" OR all:"scene text recognition" OR all:"handwritten text recognition" OR all:"information extraction") AND (cat:cs.CV OR cat:cs.AI OR cat:cs.CL OR cat:eess.IV)

synthesis.executive_summary: 今日研究集中于多模态路由、文档分类基准、OCR评估与检索增强系统。LatentRouter和Pre-Route分别展示了通过反事实预测和结构化推理实现高效模型选择与策略路由；MMM-Bench建立了多层次文档分类标准；Chronicles-OCR填补了历史文字感知空白；BICR提出轻量置信度估计方案。趋势上，路由机制与检索增强成为提升系统效率的关键，同时基准与评估工具规范化。
synthesis.trend_summary: 路由机制（模型路由、策略路由）与检索增强持续融合，通过提前预测或元数据推理优化资源分配；文档理解向多领域、多层次、多模态发展，且注重历史与生物等专业场景；置信度估计与可解释性成为提升系统可靠性的热点。
common_innovations: 通过反事实预测或盲图对比等训练前/推理前机制提升系统决策效率与可靠性
构建多层次、多领域、多模态的标准化基准以推动文档智能发展
利用元数据或潜在表示进行路由，实现成本与性能的动态平衡
在检索增强中引入答案空间感知，提高重排序与生成准确率
开发轻量探针实现零额外成本的可解释性分析
future_directions: 研究多步推理场景下的动态路由，结合在线学习更新模型能力表示
扩展历史OCR基准至其他文字系统，并增加多物理介质与语义推理联合评估
开发可解释且透明的AI工具，设计验证机制以减轻研究人员负担
探索基于弱监督或半监督的多层次文档分类，适应动态层次结构
将答案空间感知的重排序推广至更多语言与领域，降低系统复杂度
研究跨句联合证据的生物文献推理，解决证据类型混淆问题
engineering_takeaways: LatentRouter提供高效多模型选择方案，可平衡资源受限场景的性能与成本
Pre-Route的主动路由框架直接优化RAG/LC选择系统资源调度
MMM-Bench为工业级多模态文档分类提供可复用的标准基准
BICR的置信度探针轻量且通用，可嵌入现有LVLM系统
Chronicles-OCR数据集助力数字人文与历史文档OCR系统评估
Qwen RAG流水线的分块与重排序策略可直接复用于竞赛与生产环境

papers.title: LatentRouter: Can We Choose the Right Multimodal Model Before Seeing Its Answer?
papers.summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have heterogeneous strengths across OCR, chart understanding, spatial reasoning, visual question answering, cost, and latency. Effective MLLM routing therefore requires more than estimating query difficulty: a router must match the multimodal requirements of the current image-question input with the capabilities of each candidate model. We propose LatentRouter, a router that formulates MLLM routing as counterfactual multimodal utility prediction. Given an image-question query, LatentRouter extracts learned multimodal routing capsules, represents each candidate MLLM with a model capability token, and performs latent communication between these states to estimate how each model would perform if selected. A distributional outcome head predicts model-specific counterfactual quality, while a bounded capsule correction refines close decisions without allowing residual signals to dominate the prediction. The resulting utility-based policy supports performance-oriented and performance-cost routing, and handles changing candidate pools through shared per-model scoring with availability masking. Experiments on MMR-Bench and VL-RouterBench show that LatentRouter outperforms fixed-model, feature-level, and learned-router baselines. Additional analyses show that the gains are strongest on multimodal task groups where model choice depends on visual, layout-sensitive, or reasoning-oriented requirements, and that latent communication is the main contributor to the improvement. The code is available at: https://github.com/LabRAI/LatentRouter.
papers.zh_summary: 提出LatentRouter，一种通过反事实多模态效用预测来路由多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的方法。它提取多模态路由胶囊，表示每个候选模型的能力令牌，并通过潜在通信估计各模型的性能。支持面向性能和性能-成本的路由，能处理动态候选池。在MMR-Bench和VL-RouterBench上优于基线。
papers.novelty: 将MLLM路由建模为反事实效用预测，引入潜在通信机制和分布性结果头。
innovation_points: 反事实多模态效用预测框架
多模态路由胶囊与模型能力令牌
潜在通信机制
分布性结果头与有界胶囊校正
papers.limitations: 仅在现有基准上评估，未在真实部署中验证；路由策略可能对动态模型池的适应性有限。
improvement_directions: 研究更轻量的路由实现
探索多步推理场景下的路由
结合在线学习动态更新模型能力表示
papers.engineering_value: 提供一种高效的多模型选择方案，有助于在资源受限场景下平衡性能和成本。
papers.why_relevant: 直接解决OCR场景中多模型路由问题，提升下游任务效率。

papers.title: Chronicles-OCR: A Cross-Temporal Perception Benchmark for the Evolutionary Trajectory of Chinese Characters
papers.summary: Vision Large Language Models (VLLMs) have achieved remarkable success in modern text-rich visual understanding. However, their perceptual robustness in the face of the continuous morphological evolution of historical writing systems remains largely unexplored. Existing ancient text datasets typically focus on isolated historical periods, failing to capture the systematic visual distribution shifts spanning thousands of years. To bridge this gap and empower Digital Humanities, we introduce Chronicles-OCR, the first comprehensive benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the cross-temporal visual perception capabilities of VLLMs across the complete evolutionary trajectory of Chinese characters, known as the Seven Chinese Scripts. Curated in collaboration with top-tier institutional domain experts, the dataset comprises 2,800 strictly balanced images encompassing highly diverse physical media, ranging from tortoise shells to paper-based calligraphy. To accommodate the drastic morphological and topological variations across different historical stages, we propose a novel Stage-Adaptive Annotation Paradigm. Based on this, Chronicles-OCR formulates four rigorous quantitative tasks: cross-period character spotting, fine-grained archaic character recognition via visual referring, ancient text parsing, and script classification. By isolating visual perception from semantic reasoning, Chronicles-OCR provides an authoritative platform to expose the limitations of current VLLMs, paving the way for robust, evolution-aware historical text perception. Chronicles-OCR is publicly available at https://github.com/VirtualLUOUCAS/Chronicles-OCR.
papers.zh_summary: 提出Chronicles-OCR，首个评估视觉大语言模型（VLLMs）跨时间视觉感知能力的基准，涵盖汉字七体演化轨迹。数据集包含2800张图像，由领域专家标注，并设计阶段自适应标注范式与四个定量任务。
papers.novelty: 首个系统评估VLLMs对汉字历史形态演化感知能力的基准，引入阶段自适应标注范式。
innovation_points: 跨时间感知基准Chronicles-OCR
阶段自适应标注范式
四个定量任务设计
覆盖7000年汉字演化轨迹
papers.limitations: 数据集规模较小（2800张），可能无法覆盖所有历史变体；仅针对汉字。
improvement_directions: 扩展至其他文字系统
增加更多历史时期和物理介质
结合语义推理与视觉感知联合评估
papers.engineering_value: 为数字人文和历史文档OCR提供评估平台与数据资源。
papers.why_relevant: 专门针对历史文字OCR的跨时间鲁棒性评估，对古文字识别有直接意义。

papers.title: Useful for Exploration, Risky for Precision: Evaluating AI Tools in Academic Research
papers.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) tools are being incorporated into scientific research workflows with the potential to enhance efficiency in tasks such as document analysis, question answering (Q&A), and literature search. However, system outputs are often difficult to verify, lack transparency in their generation and remain prone to errors. Suitable benchmarks are needed to document and evaluate arising issues. Nevertheless, existing benchmarking approaches are not adequately capturing human-centered criteria such as usability, interpretability, and integration into research workflows. To address this gap, the present work proposes and applies a benchmarking framework combining human-centered and computer-centered metrics to evaluate AI-based Q&A and literature review tools for research use. The findings suggest that Q&A tools can offer valuable overviews and generally accurate summaries; however, they are not always reliable for precise information extraction. Explainable AI (xAI) accuracy was particularly low, meaning highlighted source passages frequently failed to correspond to generated answers. This shifted the burden of validation back onto the researcher. Literature review tools supported exploratory searches but showed low reproducibility, limited transparency regarding chosen sources and databases, and inconsistent source quality, making them unsuitable for systematic reviews. A comparison of these tool groups reveals a similar pattern: while AI tools can enhance efficiency in the early stages of the research workflow and shallow tasks, their outputs still require human verification. The findings underscore the importance of explainability features to enhance transparency, verification efficiency and careful integration of AI tools into researchers' workflows. Further, human-centered evaluation remains an important concern to ensure practical applicability.
papers.zh_summary: 评估AI问答与文献综述工具在学术研究中的可用性、可解释性和集成度。发现问答工具对精确信息提取不可靠，xAI准确率低；文献综述工具探索支持好但可重复性和透明度差。
papers.novelty: 结合人机与计算指标评估AI工具在研究工作流中的适用性。
innovation_points: 结合人类中心与计算指标的评估框架
系统评估AI问答和文献综述工具
揭示xAI准确率低的问题
papers.limitations: 样本量有限；聚焦特定AI工具，可能不具普适性。
improvement_directions: 改进AI工具的可解释性和准确性
设计更透明、可重复的文献综述工具
开发验证机制以减轻研究人员负担
papers.engineering_value: 为研究人员选择和使用AI工具提供指导，推动工具改进。
papers.why_relevant: 间接相关，但其中的文档分析评估方法对OCR工具质量评估有参考价值。

papers.title: Multi-domain Multi-modal Document Classification Benchmark with a Multi-level Taxonomy
papers.summary: Document classification forms the backbone of modern enterprise content management, yet existing benchmarks remain trapped in oversimplified paradigms -- single domain settings with flat label structures -- that bear little resemblance to the hierarchical, multi-modal, and cross-domain nature of real-world business documents. This gap not only misrepresents practical complexity but also stifles progress toward industrially viable document intelligence. To bridge this gap, we construct the first Multi-level, Multi-domain, Multi-modal document classification Benchmark (MMM-Bench). MMM-Bench includes (1) a deeply hierarchical taxonomy spanning five levels that capture the authentic organizational logic of business documentation; and (2) 5,990 real-world multi-modal documents meticulously curated from 12 commercial domains in Alibaba. Each document is manually annotated with a complete hierarchical path by domain experts. We establish comprehensive baselines on MMM-Bench, which consists of open-weight models and API-based models. Through systematic experiments, we identify four fundamental challenges within MMM-Bench and propose corresponding insights. To provide a solid foundation for advancing research in multi-level, multi-domain document classification, we release all of the data and the evaluation toolkit of MMM-Bench at https://github.com/MMMDC-Bench/MMMDC-Bench.
papers.zh_summary: 构建首个多层次、多领域、多模态文档分类基准MMM-Bench，包含五级层次分类法、来自阿里12个商业领域的5990份真实多模态文档，并由专家标注完整层次路径。评估多种基线模型并识别四个基本挑战。
papers.novelty: 首个结合多层次分类、跨领域和多模态的文档分类基准。
innovation_points: 多层次（5级）分类法
多领域（12个商业领域）多模态数据集
专家标注的完整层次路径
系统实验与挑战识别
papers.limitations: 数据来自单一企业（阿里），可能引入领域偏差；分类层次固定，不易扩展。
improvement_directions: 引入更多样化的文档来源
研究动态层次结构学习
探索弱监督或半监督标注方法
papers.engineering_value: 为多模态文档分类提供标准基准，推动工业级文档智能发展。
papers.why_relevant: 直接针对文档分类，与OCR下游任务紧密相关。

papers.title: Route Before Retrieve: Activating Latent Routing Abilities of LLMs for RAG vs. Long-Context Selection
papers.summary: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) have expanded the context window to beyond 128K tokens, enabling long-document understanding and multi-source reasoning. A key challenge, however, lies in choosing between retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and long-context (LC) strategies: RAG is efficient but constrained by retrieval quality, while LC supports global reasoning at higher cost and with position sensitivity. Existing methods such as Self-Route adopt failure-driven fallback from RAG to LC, but remain passive, inefficient, and hard to interpret. We propose Pre-Route, a proactive routing framework that performs structured reasoning before answering. Using lightweight metadata (e.g., document type, length, initial snippet), Pre-Route enables task analysis, coverage estimation, and information-need prediction, producing explainable and cost-efficient routing decisions. Our study shows three key findings: (i) LLMs possess latent routing ability that can be reliably elicited with guidelines, allowing single-sample performance to approach that of multi-sample (Best-of-N) results; (ii) linear probes reveal that structured prompts sharpen the separability of the "optimal routing dimension" in representation space; and (iii) distillation transfers this reasoning structure to smaller models for lightweight deployment. Experiments on LaRA (in-domain) and LongBench-v2 (OOD) confirm that Pre-Route outperforms Always-RAG, Always-LC, and Self-Route baselines, achieving superior overall cost-effectiveness.
papers.zh_summary: 提出Pre-Route，一种主动路由框架，在回答前通过结构化推理（基于元数据）决定使用检索增强生成（RAG）还是长上下文（LC），实现可解释且成本高效的路由。在LaRA和LongBench-v2上优于Always-RAG、Always-LC和Self-Route。
papers.novelty: 证明LLM具有潜在路由能力，并设计主动推理框架实现最佳策略选择。
innovation_points: 主动路由框架Pre-Route
利用元数据进行结构化推理
揭示LLM的潜在路由能力
蒸馏到小模型实现轻量部署
papers.limitations: 元数据可能不完全代表任务难度；依赖初始片段质量。
improvement_directions: 研究更丰富的元数据表示
探索动态元数据获取策略
将路由与模型在线学习结合
papers.engineering_value: 提升RAG/LC选择效率，对文档处理系统的资源优化有直接价值。
papers.why_relevant: 涉及文档检索与理解策略选择，对OCR后处理流程优化有借鉴意义。

papers.title: Concordance Comparison as a Means of Assembling Local Grammars
papers.summary: Named Entity Recognition for person names is an important but non-trivial task in information extraction. This article uses a tool that compares the concordances obtained from two local grammars (LG) and highlights the differences. We used the results as an aid to select the best of a set of LGs. By analyzing the comparisons, we observed relationships of inclusion, intersection and disjunction within each pair of LGs, which helped us to assemble those that yielded the best results. This approach was used in a case study on extraction of person names from texts written in Portuguese. We applied the enhanced grammar to the Gold Collection of the Second HAREM. The F-Measure obtained was 76.86, representing a gain of 6 points in relation to the state-of-the-art for Portuguese.
papers.zh_summary: 提出通过比对索引行差异来组合局部语法的方法，用于人物命名实体识别。在葡萄牙语语料上取得比当前最佳高6个百分点的F1值（76.86）。
papers.novelty: 利用索引行对比分析局部语法间的包含、相交和析取关系，辅助语法组装。
innovation_points: 索引行对比辅助语法组装方法
通过关系分析选择最佳局部语法
在葡萄牙语命名实体识别上提升6个F1点
papers.limitations: 仅针对葡萄牙语人名识别；依赖已有局部语法集合。
improvement_directions: 推广到其他语言和命名实体类型
自动发现局部语法关系
结合深度学习特征进行增强
papers.engineering_value: 提供一种可解释的语法优化方法，适用于传统NER系统。
papers.why_relevant: 涉及文本中的命名实体识别，是OCR后处理的重要环节。

papers.title: Qwen Goes Brrr: Off-the-Shelf RAG for Ukrainian Multi-Domain Document Understanding
papers.summary: We participated in the Fifth UNLP shared task on multi-domain document understanding, where systems must answer Ukrainian multiple-choice questions from PDF collections and localize the supporting document and page. We propose a retrieval-augmented pipeline built around three ideas: contextual chunking of PDFs, question-aware dense retrieval and reranking conditioned on both the question and answer options, and constrained answer generation from a small set of reranked passages. Our final system uses Qwen3-Embedding-8B for retrieval, a fine-tuned Qwen3-Reranker-8B for passage ranking, and Qwen3-32B for answer selection. On a held-out split, reranking improves Recall@1 from 0.6957 to 0.7935, while using the top-2 reranked passages raises answer accuracy from 0.9348 to 0.9674. Our best leaderboard run reached 0.9452 on the public leaderboard and 0.9598 on the private leaderboard. Our results suggest that, under strict code-competition constraints, preserving document structure and making relevance estimation aware of the answer space are more effective than adding complex downstream heuristics.
papers.zh_summary: 本文参与第五次UNLP共享任务，针对乌克兰语多领域文档理解（从PDF集合中回答选择题并定位证据文档和页面），提出一种检索增强流水线：包含上下文分块、问题感知稠密检索与重排序（同时考虑问题和选项），以及基于重排序段落的有约束答案生成。最终系统使用Qwen3-Embedding-8B检索、微调Qwen3-Reranker-8B重排序、Qwen3-32B选择答案。在保留集上重排序将Recall@1从0.6957提升到0.7935，使用top-2重排序段落准确率从0.9348提升到0.9674。公共排行榜0.9452，私有排行榜0.9598。结果表明，在严格代码竞赛约束下，保留文档结构并使相关性估计感知答案空间比添加复杂下游启发式方法更有效。
papers.novelty: 针对文档理解问答任务，提出在重排序阶段显式利用答案选项信息，而非仅依赖问题-段落匹配。
innovation_points: 提出上下文分块策略，保留文档结构（如章节、段落边界）；
设计问题感知的稠密检索与重排序，重排序模型同时以问题和所有选项作为条件；
基于重排序后的小规模段落集合进行约束答案生成，避免全文档处理。
papers.limitations: 仅针对乌克兰语，可能在其他语言上泛化性未知；依赖多个专用模型（检索、重排序、生成），系统复杂度高；在低资源场景下重排序模型需要微调，训练数据需求可能受限。
improvement_directions: 探索更轻量的端到端方法，减少对多个独立模型的依赖；
将重排序阶段的答案空间感知推广到更多语言和领域；
研究如何在不增加推理代价的情况下融合文档结构信息到检索和重排序中。
papers.engineering_value: 高，提供了一套可直接复用的检索增强文档理解流水线，尤其在竞赛等严格约束下具有实用参考价值。
papers.why_relevant: OCR文档解析领域中常涉及从结构化PDF中提取信息并回答问题的任务，该工作关于上下文分块、检索-重排序-生成流水线的设计思想可直接迁移。

papers.title: PlantMarkerBench: A Multi-Species Benchmark for Evidence-Grounded Plant Marker Reasoning
papers.summary: Cell-type-specific marker genes are fundamental to plant biology, yet existing resources primarily rely on curated databases or high-throughput studies without explicitly modeling the supporting evidence found in scientific literature. We introduce PlantMarkerBench, a multi-species benchmark for evaluating literature-grounded plant marker evidence interpretation from full-text biological papers. PlantMarkerBench is constructed using a modular curation pipeline integrating large-scale literature retrieval, hybrid search, species-aware biological grounding, structured evidence extraction, and targeted human review. The benchmark spans four plant species -- Arabidopsis, maize, rice, and tomato -- and contains 5,550 sentence-level evidence instances annotated for marker-evidence validity, evidence type, and support strength. We define two benchmark tasks: determining whether a candidate sentence provides valid marker evidence for a gene-cell-type pair, and classifying the evidence into expression, localization, function, indirect, or negative categories. We benchmark diverse open-weight and closed-source language models across species and prompting strategies. Although frontier models achieve relatively strong performance on direct expression evidence, performance drops substantially on functional, indirect, and weak-support evidence, with evidence-type confusion emerging as a dominant failure mode. Open-weight models additionally exhibit elevated false-positive rates under ambiguous biological contexts. PlantMarkerBench provides a challenging and reproducible evaluation framework for literature-grounded biological evidence attribution and supports future research on trustworthy scientific information extraction and AI-assisted plant biology.
papers.zh_summary: 本文提出PlantMarkerBench，一个多物种基准数据集，用于评估从全科学生物论文中基于文献证据解读植物细胞类型标记基因。通过模块化策展流水线构建，包含大规模文献检索、混合搜索、物种感知生物基础、结构化证据提取和人工审核，涵盖拟南芥、玉米、水稻、番茄四个物种，共5550个句子级证据实例，标注了标记证据有效性、证据类型和支持强度。定义两个任务：判断句子是否为有效的标记证据，以及分类证据类型（表达、定位、功能、间接或阴性）。基准测试了多种开源和闭源语言模型，发现前沿模型在直接表达证据上表现较好，但在功能、间接和弱支持证据上性能大幅下降，证据类型混淆是主要失败模式。开源模型在模糊生物上下文中假阳性率较高。
papers.novelty: 首个面向植物生物学的、基于文献证据的标记基因推理基准，强调证据类型细粒度分类。
innovation_points: 构建了多物种、细粒度标注的植物标记基因证据数据集；
模块化策展流水线集成多种检索和证据提取技术；
定义了两阶段任务（有效性判断+类型分类），覆盖表达、定位、功能等证据类型。
papers.limitations: 仅包含四个植物物种，覆盖面有限；证据实例来自句子级别，可能忽略跨句联合证据；基准任务难度较高，当前模型表现有限，尤其是复杂性证据。
improvement_directions: 扩展更多物种和更多样化的证据类型（如实验条件依赖的证据）；
发展能够理解跨句联合证据和生物语境推理的方法；
探索证据类型混淆的解决方案，例如通过多任务学习或结构化预测。
papers.engineering_value: 中高，为植物生物学领域提供了可重复的评估框架，有助于推动文献挖掘和知识库构建工具的发展。
papers.why_relevant: OCR文档解析中常需从科学文献中抽取结构化知识，该工作关于证据类型分类和细粒度推理的思路可用于文档解析后的信息抽取和验证。

papers.title: Grounded or Guessing? LVLM Confidence Estimation via Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking
papers.summary: Large vision-language models suffer from visual ungroundedness: they can produce a fluent, confident, and even correct response driven entirely by language priors, with the image contributing nothing to the prediction. Existing confidence estimation methods cannot detect this, as they observe model behavior under normal inference with no mechanism to determine whether a prediction was shaped by the image or by text alone. We introduce BICR (Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking), a model-agnostic confidence estimation framework that makes this contrast explicit during training by extracting hidden states from a frozen LVLM twice: once with the real image-question pair, and once with the image blacked out while the question is held fixed. A lightweight probe is trained on the real-image hidden state and regularized by a ranking loss that penalizes higher confidence on the blacked-out view, teaching it to treat visual grounding as a signal of reliability at zero additional inference cost. Evaluated across five modern LVLMs and seven baselines on a benchmark covering visual question answering, object hallucination detection, medical imaging, and financial document understanding, BICR achieves the best cross-LVLM average on both calibration and discrimination simultaneously, with statistically significant discrimination gains robust to cluster-aware analysis at 4-18x fewer parameters than the strongest probing baseline.
papers.zh_summary: 本文指出大视觉语言模型存在视觉无根基问题：即使图像信息未被使用，模型仍能产生流畅、自信甚至正确的回应（仅依赖语言先验）。现有置信度估计方法无法检测此现象。作者提出BICR（盲图对比排序），一种模型无关的置信度估计框架。训练时，对冻结的LVLM提取两次隐藏状态：一次用真实图像-问题对，一次将图像变黑但问题不变。训练轻量探针基于真实图像隐藏状态，并通过排序损失正则化，惩罚在盲图上给出高置信度，使其学会将视觉基础视为可靠性信号，且推理时无额外代价。在五个LVLM和七个基线、覆盖VQA、物体幻觉检测、医学影像和金融文档理解的基准上，BICR在标定和区分度上同时取得最佳平均性能，区分度增益具有统计显著性，且参数比最强探针基线少4-18倍。
papers.novelty: 提出通过对比有无视觉输入时模型内部表示来显式检测视觉基础程度的新颖训练范式，用于置信度估计。
innovation_points: 提出盲图对比训练策略，利用真实图像-黑图对的隐藏状态差异训练探针；
设计排序损失惩罚在黑图上输出高置信度，使探针学会感知视觉基础程度；
实现零额外推理代价的置信度估计，且在多个LVLM和任务上表现最优。
papers.limitations: 探针训练需要带真实图像和黑图的数据，对某些无法获取原始图像的场景（如加密图像）可能受限；依赖冻结LVLM的隐藏状态，可能无法捕捉更深层表示。
improvement_directions: 探索无需黑图的数据增强方法，扩大应用场景；
将BICR扩展到视频或3D理解等更复杂的多模态任务；
研究如何利用视觉基础信号改进模型生成质量，而不仅仅是估计置信度。
papers.engineering_value: 高，提供了一个轻量、高效、通用性强的置信度估计模块，可直接嵌入现有LVLM系统，提升可靠性。
papers.why_relevant: OCR文档解析中常使用LVLM处理图像文档，该工作关于检测模型是否真正依赖图像信息的方法，有助于识别和规避文档理解中的语言先验偏差。
